ucs.title: [LLM] Andariel (Onyx Sleet) abuse of Innorix Agent / Korean security plug-ins for lateral tool transfer
ucs.description: Hunts the actor-specific TTP that Microsoft attributed to Onyx Sleet/Andariel: abusing the Innorix Agent file-transfer client (and adjacent Korean PKI/security plug-ins like INISAFE, TouchEn, MagicLine — pre-installed on most South Korean enterprise endpoints) as a delivery vector for TigerRAT, DTrack, NukeSped and Volgmer. Detects when these Korean-locale agents spawn LOLBins or scripting hosts, which is the documented post-exploitation chain unique to this DPRK cluster targeting ROK government/finance/defense.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, values(Processes.process) as ChildCmd, values(Processes.parent_process) as ParentCmd, values(Processes.process_path) as ChildPath, min(_time) as FirstSeen, max(_time) as LastSeen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("INNORIXAgent.exe","INISAFEWebEX.exe","INNORIX_Agent.exe","inisafecrosswebexsvc.exe","TouchEnNxKey.exe","MagicLine4NX.exe","VeraportGAgent.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\INNORIX\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\INISAFE\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\TouchEn*\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\MagicLine*\\*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe") AND NOT Processes.user="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | sort - LastSeen
ucs.kql:
// Andariel / Onyx Sleet — Innorix & Korean PKI plug-in abuse
let KoreanAgentParents = dynamic(["innorixagent.exe","innorix_agent.exe","inisafewebex.exe","inisafecrosswebexsvc.exe","touchennxkey.exe","magicline4nx.exe","veraportgagent.exe"]);
let KoreanAgentPaths   = dynamic([@"\INNORIX\", @"\INISAFE\", @"\TouchEn", @"\MagicLine", @"\Veraport"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (KoreanAgentParents)
     or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (KoreanAgentPaths)
| where tolower(FileName) in (SuspiciousChildren)
     or FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildFile   = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Andariel / Onyx Sleet — Innorix & Korean PKI plug-in abuse (Sentinel via 4688)
let KoreanAgents = dynamic(["innorixagent.exe","innorix_agent.exe","inisafewebex.exe","inisafecrosswebexsvc.exe","touchennxkey.exe","magicline4nx.exe","veraportgagent.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (KoreanAgents)
     or ParentProcessName has_any (@"\INNORIX\", @"\INISAFE\", @"\TouchEn", @"\MagicLine", @"\Veraport")
| where ChildLeaf in (SuspiciousChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, TokenElevationType, IpAddress
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Andariel Onyx Sleet - Korean Security Plug-in Abuse for Tool Transfer
id: 7d6b8c1e-1f1a-4f7e-9c1f-andariel-innorix
status: experimental
description: Detects Andariel/Onyx Sleet abusing Korean-locale endpoint agents (Innorix Agent, INISAFE, TouchEn, MagicLine, Veraport) as launching parents for LOLBins / scripting hosts - documented post-exploit chain for TigerRAT, DTrack, NukeSped delivery against ROK targets.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/07/25/onyx-sleet-uses-array-of-malware-to-gather-intelligence-for-north-korea/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0138/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g0138
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_korean_agent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\INNORIXAgent.exe'
      - '\INNORIX_Agent.exe'
      - '\INISAFEWebEX.exe'
      - '\inisafecrosswebexsvc.exe'
      - '\TouchEnNxKey.exe'
      - '\MagicLine4NX.exe'
      - '\VeraportGAgent.exe'
  parent_korean_path:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '\INNORIX\'
      - '\INISAFE\'
      - '\TouchEn'
      - '\MagicLine'
      - '\Veraport'
  child_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  condition: (parent_korean_agent or parent_korean_path) and child_lolbin
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software updates pushed by IT via Innorix/INISAFE management consoles (baseline 30d before alerting)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Innorix Agent, INISAFE, TouchEn, MagicLine and Veraport are Korean-only PKI/file-transfer plug-ins effectively absent from non-ROK estates. Andariel/Onyx Sleet are the primary publicly-attributed actor that weaponises these agents (Microsoft Threat Intelligence, July 2024; AhnLab ASEC reporting). A western APT or e-crime crew triggering this rule is so unlikely that the parent-process predicate is

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Andariel DTrack/TigerRAT host-recon command burst (T1049 + T1057 fan-out)
ucs.description: Catches Andariel's signature post-implant reconnaissance: DTrack and TigerRAT shell out a tight burst of native Windows discovery binaries (ipconfig /all, systeminfo, tasklist /v, whoami /all, net view, netstat -ano, route print, arp -a, query user, nltest /dclist) within minutes from a single parent PID. The actor's two top-listed MITRE techniques are T1049 + T1057 — this UC operationalises that pairing as a single-event-cluster detection.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, dc(Processes.process_name) as DistinctTools, values(Processes.process_name) as Tools, values(Processes.process) as Cmds, min(_time) as FirstSeen, max(_time) as LastSeen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","hostname.exe") AND NOT Processes.user="*$" AND NOT Processes.parent_process_name IN ("ccmexec.exe","sccm.exe","mmc.exe","sense.exe","MsSense.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id Processes.parent_process_name _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where DistinctTools >= 4 | sort - LastSeen
ucs.kql:
// Andariel / DTrack-TigerRAT recon burst
let WindowMin = 5m;
let DiscoveryBins = dynamic(["ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","hostname.exe"]);
let BenignParents = dynamic(["ccmexec.exe","sccm.exe","mmc.exe","sense.exe","mssense.exe","intunemanagementextension.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where tolower(FileName) in (DiscoveryBins)
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) !in (BenignParents)
| summarize
      DistinctTools = dcount(FileName),
      Tools         = make_set(FileName, 20),
      SampleCmds    = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 20),
      ParentImage   = any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath),
      ParentCmd     = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
      ParentSHA     = any(InitiatingProcessSHA256),
      FirstSeen     = min(Timestamp),
      LastSeen      = max(Timestamp),
      Hits          = count()
      by DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessId,
         InitiatingProcessFileName, bin(Timestamp, WindowMin)
| where DistinctTools >= 4   // 4+ distinct discovery LOLBins inside one 5-min window from same parent PID = DTrack/TigerRAT signature
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Andariel / DTrack-TigerRAT recon burst (Sentinel via 4688)
let WindowMin = 5m;
let DiscoveryBins = dynamic(["ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","hostname.exe"]);
let BenignParents  = dynamic(["ccmexec.exe","sccm.exe","mmc.exe","sense.exe","mssense.exe","intunemanagementextension.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])),
         ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ChildLeaf in (DiscoveryBins)
| where ParentLeaf !in (BenignParents)
| summarize
      DistinctTools = dcount(ChildLeaf),
      Tools         = make_set(ChildLeaf, 20),
      SampleCmds    = make_set(CommandLine, 20),
      ParentName    = any(ParentProcessName),
      FirstSeen     = min(TimeGenerated),
      LastSeen      = max(TimeGenerated),
      Hits          = count()
      by Computer, Account, ProcessId, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMin)
| where DistinctTools >= 4
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Andariel's MITRE profile foregrounds T1049 + T1057 — and reverse-engineering of DTrack (Kaspersky, 2019; ESET, 2022), TigerRAT (KrCERT, 2022) and the more recent Dora RAT samples shows a hard-coded chain of 5–8 of these exact discovery binaries fired within a single child shell context immediately after implant ping-back. Most administrative tooling (SCCM, Intune, MMC) and human admins do not run
